hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific ECL2/TM5 complementarity, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will reduce off-target SSTR3/5 binding via steric differentiation.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current protocol generates candidates with poor structural quality and invalid docking scores despite strong selectivity. The focus should shift to improving structural validity while maintaining selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: High clash scores likely contribute to poor structural quality and zero docking scores. Reducing the clash threshold will enforce better geometry and improve model quality.
The low pass rate suggests the search space is too constrained. Increasing the number of candidates will improve diversity and the likelihood of generating high-quality models.
